options.text: Oh no! That's a typo—I live at 125. Can you fix it?
I don't care which number it is.
You should change your passport instead.

content.explanation: 正确答案使用了礼貌承认错误并请求修正的表达，符合核对信息场景的沟通礼仪。选项1错误地坚持错误信息；选项3态度冷漠，不专业；选项4推卸责任，不符合服务场景的协作原则。
content.speakerText: Hmm, your address says '123 Maple St'—but your passport says '125'.
content.speakerTextCn: 嗯，你的地址写的是枫树街123号，但护照写的是125号。

options.text: I'm not sure. Maybe it's Biology.
Actually, I switched to Biochem last week. Please update it!
Why are you checking my major? It's none of your business.
Biochemistry is too hard, so I changed back to Biology.

content.explanation: 正确答案清晰说明变更事实并礼貌请求更新，符合场景要求。选项1态度模糊，未明确回应；选项3语气粗鲁，不适用于正式场合；选项4虽为真实情况，但表达消极且未请求修正，不符合服务流程。
content.speakerText: Your major's listed as 'Biology'—did you mean 'Biochemistry'?
content.speakerTextCn: 你的专业写的是生物学，你是想写生物化学吗？

options.text: It doesn't matter. Both are fine.
Oh, I made a mistake—I use 'Chen' officially. Please correct it.
I don't remember. Can you decide for me?
Chan is my nickname. I prefer that one.

content.explanation: 正确答案明确指出正确拼写并请求修正，体现专业性。选项1回避责任，不解决问题；选项3推卸决定权，不符合个人责任原则；选项4虽真实，但未说明官方记录应使用哪个，未完成核对任务。
content.speakerText: I see your last name is spelled 'Chen' here, but your email signature says 'Chan'. Which one is correct?
content.speakerTextCn: 我看到你的姓氏这里拼写为 'Chen'，但你的邮件签名是 'Chan'。哪个是正确的？
